step_by_step_thinking: Colistin belongs to the polymyxin family, specifically categorized under "polymyxins" — a group explicitly described as antibiotics targeting gram-negative bacterial infections. Multiple references confirm that colistin is FDA-approved for treating severe infections due to multidrug-resistant gram-negative pathogens like *Enterobacteriaceae*, *Pseudomonas aeruginosa*, and *Acinetobacter baumannii*. This classification establishes colistin’s role as a clinically utilized antibiotic.
raw_response:
Colistin belongs to the polymyxin family, specifically categorized under "polymyxins" — a group explicitly described as antibiotics targeting gram-negative bacterial infections. Multiple references confirm that colistin is FDA-approved for treating severe infections due to multidrug-resistant gram-negative pathogens like *Enterobacteriaceae*, *Pseudomonas aeruginosa*, and *Acinetobacter baumannii*. This classification establishes colistin’s role as a clinically utilized antibiotic.
\boxed{A}
